title: Color picker swatch buttons don't have distinct accessible names for screen readers
author: saloni8780
body:
## Description
The color swatch buttons in the color picker (both "Top Picks" and "Custom Colors" 
sections) don't expose the specific color they represent to screen readers.

- In `TopPicks.tsx`, swatch buttons only set `title={color}` — screen readers don't 
  reliably announce the `title` attribute, so each button is announced simply as "button".
- In `CustomColorList.tsx`, swatch buttons do set `aria-label`, but it's passed the 
  generic `label` prop (e.g. "Background color"), identical across every swatch — so a 
  screen reader user tabbing through 5 custom colors hears "Background color, button" 
  five times with no way to distinguish which color is which.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Open the color picker (e.g. background or stroke color).
2. Turn on a screen reader (VoiceOver/NVDA) or inspect the DOM/accessibility tree.
3. Tab through the "Top Picks" and "Custom Colors" swatches.

## Expected Behavior
Each swatch button should announce its specific color value, e.g. 
`aria-label="Color: #ffec99"`.

## Actual Behavior
- Top Picks swatches have no aria-label at all.
- Custom Colors swatches share one generic aria-label across all swatches.

## Suggested Fix
Add a per-swatch `aria-label` (e.g. `` `${label}: ${color}` ``) in both `TopPicks.tsx` 
and `CustomColorList.tsx`.

title: feature : Simple preference color saver for different  Tools
author: RKV-07
body:
---
 ### **Context** : Primarily thank you for this wonderful project . it help me maintain my flowcharts and descriptions intact.  but seeing the issues got me concerned whether issue will ever close. 
---
 ### **Problem** : there is a persistence of colors while changing the tools ( arrow, write, shapes, etc). it would be clear if we can  have different color preferences for different tool with a default one at starting.
---
### **Possible solution ?** : Implementing tool-specific color persistence in Excalidraw involves mapping custom colors to specific tools in the appState and updating these mappings on tool-selection events. This approach ensures that manual color changes are saved per tool, allowing the application to maintain independent color settings for items like arrows or rectangles rather than relying on a single global style.
-  __the state interface acts as__  : Update the state holding activeTool to track toolColors: Record<string, string> where the key is the tool type (arrow, text, rectangle, etc.). Set your fallback default color in the initial app state.
---
### Conclusion : Thank you once again. having this feature allows people write clearly instead of changing colors everytime we use tools

title: add open-source LXGW font
author: faon-92
body:
Hello,

I would like to suggest adding support for the open-source LXGW font family as built-in fonts, espacillay LXGW Wenkai.
https://github.com/lxgw/LxgwWenKai

These fonts are widely appreciated in the CJK community, have excellent readability, and are released under the SIL Open Font License, making them suitable for inclusion in open-source projects.

Thank you for considering this suggestion, and thank you again for your great work on Excalidraw!

title: Canvas drag (hand/pan) breaks drawing tools and pointer input until page reload (XP-Pen Deco V2 on macOS)
author: Must2343
body:
Bug Description
After dragging the canvas with the hand tool (or equivalent drag-to-pan), the drawing tools (especially the pencil/freehand) stop working completely. No strokes are created, and switching tools doesn't help. The only fix is to reload the page/tab.
This happens even without zooming or using Space key — just normal canvas dragging.
Steps to Reproduce

Open Excalidraw (standalone at excalidraw.com or in Obsidian Excalidraw plugin).
Select the hand tool or drag the canvas normally.
Try to draw with the pencil tool (or any other tool).
Nothing happens — input is broken.

Expected Behavior
Drawing should work normally after panning/dragging the canvas.
Actual Behavior
Pointer/tablet input for drawing is lost until full reload.
Environment

Device: XP-Pen Deco V2
OS: macOS 26.5.2 (25F84)
Browser: Microsoft Edge (also tested in others)
Excalidraw version: latest (web)
Also reproduced in Obsidian Excalidraw plugin
Tablet driver: freshly installed ~2 days ago
Note: Works fine in Photoshop with the same tablet/driver

Additional Info

Reloading the page restores functionality temporarily.
The bug is very consistent when using the tablet for panning.
No issues with basic mouse input in some cases, but tablet stylus triggers it reliably.

title: After uploading the image to the excalicord, the image loses clarity and the colors become washed out.
author: pengfangyi91-png
body:
The original image is very sharp, with rich, vibrant colors, and the file size is quite large. However, after uploading it to Excalicord, the entire image looks as if a layer of dust has been placed over it. It becomes noticeably blurry, and the colors appear washed out and much less vibrant.

Has anyone else experienced the same issue?

title: No documentation that [shift] + drag causes 'snap' behavior
author: DustinX
body:
I can't find this information in the 'help' popup or the documentation or the onscreen tips.
It's pretty important functionality that users won't necessarily just know.

title: Forlan Pay
author: maluthdit1234-bit

title: No ability to change text shape when editing on Android tablet
author: cms103
body:
When using an Android tablet, the resize behaviour for text is to change the font size. There is no toggle to allow the shape of the text to be changed.

Other objects (e.g. rectangle) have a toggle button under actions that allows you to choose between resizing the object and the text size.

title: [Feature Request]: Improve organization and searchability of downloaded items/scripts
author: gitgud5000
body: When many downloaded items/scripts are added, the current “Downloaded” panel becomes difficult to navigate. Items are shown as a dense grid of icons with little visible metadata, and relying on hover text is slow because the label/details take time to appear. This makes it hard to quickly find and reuse a specific item once the collection grows. Pinning items to the sidebar helps only partially, because the same discoverability problem still applies there when many items are pinned. Things that would improve the experience: - Add a search/filter box. - Show item names or short labels directly under icons. - Add sorting options, such as recently used, recently downloaded, alphabetical, or category, etc. - Support grouping/folders for downloaded items. - Make hover previews/tooltips appear faster or show more useful metadata. This should help users (like me) with many downloaded items to be able to find and reuse them much faster, instead of scanning a large icon-only grid or waiting for hover labels one by one.

title: Blinking Text Cursor line not visible when center text align enabled
author: RushilJalal
body:
When typing inside a rectangle, I noticed that the blinking text cursor works normally when text align is right or left.

On switching to center, the cursor dissappears, making it confusing as to which object is selected. After typing, the cursor reappears.

Expected behavior: Cursor should blink when text align is selected as center(not working) and after typing a few words out too(working).

title: Feature Request: Add "Convert Shape" option for existing shapes
author: omavashia2005
body:
Add a context menu option that allows users to convert an existing shape into another supported shape type while preserving its position, size, styling, and content where possible.

**Example workflow:**

* Right-click a shape (e.g. a circle).
* Select **Convert to**.
* Choose a target shape (e.g. Rectangle, Diamond, Ellipse, etc.).
* The selected shape is replaced with the chosen shape type while retaining applicable properties.

This would make it much faster to iterate on diagrams without deleting and recreating shapes when experimenting with different layouts or visual representations.

title: Feature request: Export draft templates as PowerPoint files
author: miraclemanza
body:
# Feature Request: Export Draft Templates as PowerPoint

## Problem Statement
Currently, when working on draft templates in Illustrate.co, users need to make edits directly within the platform. It would be more convenient to export these drafts as PowerPoint (.pptx) files to edit them externally in Microsoft PowerPoint or other compatible software, then potentially reimport or use them elsewhere.

## Desired Functionality
- Add an "Export as PowerPoint" option for draft templates
- Preserve all formatting, layouts, and design elements when converting to .pptx format
- Support batch export functionality for multiple drafts

## Use Case
As a user, I want to:
1. Work on draft templates in Illustrate.co
2. Export them as PowerPoint files
3. Make additional edits in PowerPoint or share them with team members who use PowerPoint
4. Have the flexibility to edit presentations in external tools without being limited to the Illustrate.co platform

## Benefits
- Increased flexibility for users who prefer PowerPoint editing
- Better integration with existing PowerPoint workflows
- Easier collaboration with non-Illustrate.co users
- More versatile content output options

## Additional Context
This feature would be particularly useful for users who need to work across multiple design tools or share presentation templates with stakeholders who use standard Office applications.

title: Command palette shows a mix of two languages after switching language back and forth
author: jithu2111
body: ## Description After switching the UI language and then switching back (e.g. English → Hindi → English), opening the command palette shows command labels in a **mix of both languages** — most are in the newly selected language, but some remain in the previous one. ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Open Excalidraw (reproducible on https://excalidraw.com and on a local `master` build). 2. Change the language (e.g. English → Hindi) via the language selector. 3. Change it back (Hindi → English). 4. Open the command palette (`Cmd/Ctrl + /`). 5. Observe that some command labels are still in the previously selected language while the rest are in the current one. ## Expected Behavior All command palette labels should be shown in the currently selected language. ## Actual Behavior Labels appear in a mix of the current and previous languages (see screenshot — e.g. "जीवंत सहयोग..." and "साझा करें" remained in Hindi while the surrounding items rendered in English). ## Screenshot ## Environment - Reproducible on https://excalidraw.com and latest `master`
